codeReview.summary: The prior blocking issue (env vars not applied to spawned process) is NOT FIXED. While the `mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix` feature exists in features.json as a pending fix, the code has not been modified to pass env vars to the spawned MCP server process. The fix feature remains pending with no worker session.

issues.description: Environment variables from McpServerConfig.env are still NOT applied when spawning the server process. McpClient::connect() calls tokio::process::Command::new(&program).args(&args) but still never calls .envs() or .env(). The flow is: McpConfigManager::start_server() -> McpClient::new(server_config.to_command_string()) where env is discarded, then McpClient::connect() spawns the process without the configured environment variables. The fix has not been implemented.

Unused variable 'base_dir' computed from config_path.parent() with comment '// suppress unused warning'. This code smell remains from the original implementation - base_dir is never used for anything.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix feature mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix was created but has not been started. The worker session IDs array is empty for this fix feature. No implementation has occurred.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix with workerSessionIds: [] and status: pending

sharedStateObservations.observation: The McpClient constructor takes only server_url (command string) but doesn't accept env HashMap. This design means env cannot be passed through to the spawned process without API changes. A proper fix would require either: (1) extending McpClient::new() to accept env, or (2) storing env in McpConfigManager and applying it during start_server before calling client.connect().
sharedStateObservations.evidence: McpClient::new(server_url: impl Into<String>) has no env parameter - crates/swell-tools/src/mcp.rs:274

sharedStateObservations.observation: The McpConfigManager holds the server_config with env but doesn't use it when creating the client. McpConfigManager::start_server() at line 261-267 creates the client with just the command string, completely ignoring the env field. The fix needs to apply the env at this point.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-tools/src/mcp_config.rs:265 - client = McpClient::new(server_config.to_command_string()); env field is never passed

summary: The blocking issue from the prior review (env vars not applied to spawned process) is NOT FIXED. The code at mcp.rs:309 still spawns the process without calling .envs(). The fix feature mcp-env-vars-runtime-fix was created but remains pending with no implementation. The McpClient API would need modification to accept and apply env vars, or McpConfigManager::start_server() would need to pass env to the client before connect().
